digest.component_or_area: release pipeline and store-compatibility gate for navapbc/rebar v1.0
propositions: release.yml publishes on any human-pushed v* tag with no machine proof the tagged SHA is reviewed-and-landed main; releases should instead be initiated from protected main via workflow_dispatch gated on a required-reviewers pypi environment with OIDC trusted publishing and no tag-triggered path.

There is no committed store-format-version/required-capability record, so a v1.0 binary cannot fail closed on a store it cannot correctly interpret; the gate must ship in v1.0 and cover all mutation/publish paths (append, transition, claim, delete, archive, compaction, MCP writes, automatic repair, reconciler inbound, Jira push/reconcile).

The store gate must treat an absent record as legacy format (version 0, compatible, fail-open) with ensure-registry writing it on next covered write, fail closed only on present-but-incompatible or corrupt records, and remain rollback-safe for pre-v1.0 binaries.

python -m build's default wheel-from-sdist path bakes COMMIT = None into the published wheel and CI installs the source tree via pip install . rather than the artifact bundle, so the exact hash-verified wheel and sdist must be exercised before publication with a correct non-null source commit.

The release pipeline resolves actions, build tools, and a privileged OIDC MCP publisher from mutable references, inconsistent with the repo's SHA-pinning in gerrit-verify.yaml; every executable must use immutable references and checkouts must not persist credentials.

Removed env vars are silently ignored and removed TOML keys get only a generic typo warning; removed inputs affecting store location, write/sync gates, auth, security, or lifecycle policy must fail loud with a targeted migration message and nonzero exit.
